Title: Gibberellins orchestrate panicle architecture mediated by DELLA–KNOX signalling in rice

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: GA20-oxidation steps in both GA53→GA20 and GA12→GA9 routes
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: GA53; GA44; GA19; GA12; GA15; GA24
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: GA44; GA19; GA20; GA15; GA24; GA9
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes successive 20-oxidation steps converting GA53 to GA20 via GA44 and GA19, and GA12 to GA9 via GA15 and GA24.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursors and terminal products of the GA20ox step that supply bioactive GA biosynthesis and GA intermediate pools affecting panicle development
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Gibberellins (especially GA20 and GA9 intermediates; related to GA1/GA3/GA4 biosynthesis)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes rice panicle development, plant height, branching, and grain number
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Young panicles/inflorescences during early panicle development
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Widely expressed; higher in young leaf, leaf sheath, root, and developing panicles; detected in rachis meristem, elongated primary and secondary branch meristems, and spikelet meristems
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed during early panicle development and in vegetative organs; promoter-GFP active in rachis meristem, early/elongated primary branch meristem, and secondary branch meristem
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step in GA biosynthesis affecting panicle architecture; not explicitly established as sole rate-limiting step
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based cloning; complementation; overexpression; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; GA metabolite profiling by UPLC-MS; qRT-PCR; in situ hybridization; promoter-GFP
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes GA intermediate accumulation, panicle length, branch number, grain number, and plant height; loss of function causes small panicles and semidwarfism
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. qPA1 was cloned as SD1/OsGA20ox2 and complementation restored panicle size and plant height. 2. A nonsense mutation in the 9311 allele truncates SD1 and abolishes function. 3. CRISPR knockout of SD1 reduced panicle length, primary and secondary branches, grain number, and plant height. 4. sd1 mutants had reduced GA intermediates, especially GA9 and GA20, linking SD1 activity to GA metabolism during panicle development.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native SD1 promoter (constitutive/developmentally active in panicle and vegetative tissues); CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field growth in paddy conditions; young panicle developmental stage (<1 mm to ~1 cm) was critical for metabolic measurements
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for yield-oriented breeding and panicle architecture improvement; favorable functional SD1 alleles can be combined with GNP1 to refine panicle architecture, but loss-of-function alleles reduce yield components despite semidwarf stature
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function reduces plant height and severely decreases panicle branching and grain number; semidwarf advantage may trade off with yield architecture
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The 9311 allele showed higher promoter activity than Nipponbare, but was non-functional due to a rare nonsense mutation causing premature truncation by 47 amino acids; bioactive GA1/GA3 levels were unchanged, suggesting GA intermediates themselves or compensatory GA catabolism may be important.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Predominantly GA53-derived route according to cited functional preference
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: GA53 (preferred); also GA intermediates in GA20ox reactions
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes GA20-oxidation reactions in gibberellin biosynthesis, with reported higher affinity for GA53 than GA12.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies GA intermediate pool controlling panicle development
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes panicle branching, grain number, and plant height
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed throughout development, with much higher expression during panicle development than SD1
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in panicle organs; pGNP1:GNP1 expression did not rescue sd1 but pSD1:GNP1 did
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key GA biosynthetic contributor for panicle development; non-redundant with SD1 due to expression divergence
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; double-mutant analysis with sd1; promoter-swap complementation; subcellular localization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes primary/secondary branch number and grain number; single mutants reduce panicle traits and double mutant with sd1 shows stronger defects
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. gnp1 single mutants showed reduced branch number and grain number. 2. sd1 gnp1 double mutants had more severe panicle defects than either single mutant, indicating additive/non-redundant roles. 3. GNP1 could rescue sd1 only when expressed under the SD1 promoter, demonstrating functional divergence mainly from spatiotemporal expression differences.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native GNP1 promoter; SD1 promoter for promoter-swap construct
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful with SD1 stacking to improve panicle architecture and grain yield; promoter engineering may be more effective than coding-sequence changes for trait optimization
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockout reduces branching and grain number; combined defects with sd1 are severe
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study emphasizes non-redundancy with SD1 despite both being GA20ox paralogs; divergence is attributed mainly to promoter-driven spatiotemporal expression rather than subcellular localization.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: DELLA protein; GRAS family; N-terminal DELLA domain and C-terminal GRAS domain
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Gibberellin signaling-linked transcriptional control of panicle development
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein-protein interaction-mediated repression of KNOX class I transcriptional activation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Gibberellin status; reduced SD1-mediated GA biosynthesis leads to SLR1 accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Physically interacts with KNOX class I proteins and represses OSH1-mediated activation of downstream genes such as ASP1
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms repressive complexes with KNOX class I proteins, especially OSH1
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes appropriate panicle architecture when degraded under normal GA signaling; accumulation represses development
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Protein accumulates more strongly in young panicles of sd1 mutants
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in young panicles; accumulation increased in sd1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Western blot; yeast two-hybrid; BiFC; split-luciferase; truncation interaction assays; luciferase reporter assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a negative regulator of panicle development by repressing KNOX-mediated downstream gene activation when accumulated
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller linking reduced GA biosynthesis to repression of panicle-development gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SLR1 protein accumulated in sd1 young panicles. 2. SLR1 directly interacted with multiple KNOX class I proteins. 3. The GRAS domain of SLR1 mediated interaction with OSH1 domains. 4. Co-expression of SLR1 suppressed OSH1 activation of ASP1, establishing a GA signaling mechanism affecting panicle architecture.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating SLR1 stability or its interaction surface with KNOX proteins may help optimize panicle architecture, but excessive DELLA activity is detrimental
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Accumulation is associated with reduced panicle branching and likely reduced yield components
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Young panicle developmental stage; altered GA biosynthesis background (sd1)
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper focuses on developmental output rather than direct metabolite abundance changes; SLR1 serves as the core signaling node connecting SD1-dependent GA status to KNOX transcriptional control.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class I KNOTTED1-like homeobox (KNOX) transcription factor; KNOX1, ELK, and HOX/homeobox domains
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional activation of panicle-development downstream genes under GA signaling context
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct promoter binding and transcriptional activation, repressed by interaction with SLR1
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: GA signaling through SLR1; SD1-dependent GA status
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly binds the ASP1 promoter and activates its expression; this activation is reduced by SLR1
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with SLR1; likely functions as homodimer via HOX domain per cited knowledge
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to panicle branching and grain production by enabling GA-responsive developmental gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in shoot apical meristem, inflorescence meristem, and floral meristem; reduced in sd1 panicle tissues
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Lower transcript level in sd1 than wild type during panicle development
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; BiFC; split-luciferase; yeast one-hybrid; luciferase reporter assay; domain truncation analysis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes expression of downstream genes such as ASP1 to support panicle development; activity is repressed by SLR1 accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key downstream transcriptional activator whose sequestration by DELLA links GA status to panicle architecture
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OSH1 directly interacts with SLR1. 2. OSH1 binds the ASP1 promoter and activates transcription. 3. SLR1 represses OSH1-mediated activation of ASP1. 4. KNOX1 and HOX domains of OSH1 are involved in interaction with SLR1 GRAS domain.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for improving panicle architecture by weakening inhibitory SLR1 interaction while preserving meristem function
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Disrupting KNOX function can impair meristem maintenance and panicle formation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although several KNOX class I proteins interact with SLR1, OSH1 was the principal experimentally dissected regulator in downstream transcription assays.
